title: Primary Source Sleuth
short_description: Detective work with history: is this clue a PRIMARY source made at the time, or a SECONDARY source made later about it? Grab your magnifying glass.
description: Real historians are detectives, and the first question they ask about any clue is: where did this come from? In each case you're shown a source — a soldier's diary, a school textbook, an old photograph, a modern documentary, an ancient coin — and you decide whether it's a PRIMARY source (first-hand, from the time it happened) or a SECONDARY source (made later, telling us about it). It teaches the historical-thinking skill behind every history lesson: evaluating evidence, spotting first-hand accounts, and understanding why the source matters. Grade bands ramp from obvious objects, to letters and encyclopedias, to tricky edge cases like interviews and autobiographies. Correct verdicts and streaks earn bonus points. A free online sources, evidence and historical-thinking game for kids in grades 1–5, feeding your score and GP on OnlyGrins.
controls: Mouse / tap PRIMARY or SECONDARY for each source.
